name: subagent-factory
description: Create specialized Claude Code agents on-the-fly. Guides through agent definition file creation with proper frontmatter, effective prompts, and tool scoping. USE WHEN user says 'create agent', 'new subagent', 'make an agent for', 'build agent', 'spawn agent', or wants to define custom agents for specific tasks.
capabilities: Guides users through creating agent definition files with YAML frontmatter
Provides quick and interview workflows for agent creation
Documents complete agent schema including tools, models, and permissions
Covers Task tool parameters for subagent invocation
Includes prompt engineering patterns for effective system prompts
author: AaronAbuUsama
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. Contains only markdown files explaining agent creation patterns, YAML frontmatter schemas, and Task tool usage. All static findings (1220 external_commands, weak crypto, sensitive keywords) are false positives triggered by documentation examples in code blocks. The skill only reads its own files and provides guidance.
valueStatement: Building custom agents for specific tasks requires understanding Claude Code's agent schema, Task tool parameters, and prompt engineering patterns. This skill provides guided workflows and comprehensive reference documentation for creating effective specialized agents.
userTitle: Create specialized Claude Code agents on-demand
limitations: Does not execute agent creation - only provides guidance and templates
Requires Claude Code to be installed and configured
Does not manage agent deployment or versioning
No built-in agent testing or validation capabilities

useCases.title: Create code review agents
useCases.description: Build specialized agents for code review, security audits, or testing with custom tool access and specific checklists

useCases.title: Create deployment agents
useCases.description: Design agents for deployment workflows with appropriate permissions and integration hooks for CI/CD pipelines

useCases.title: Create team-specific agents
useCases.description: Standardize agent behavior across teams with shared agent definitions in project repositories

promptTemplates.title: Create reviewer agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the quick-create workflow to make a security-reviewer agent with Read, Grep, Glob tools, sonnet model, and focus on OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Create test runner
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the interview workflow to create a test-runner agent that executes tests, diagnoses failures, and proposes fixes with Edit access

promptTemplates.title: Create researcher
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a tech-researcher agent using WebFetch and WebSearch tools to investigate technologies and provide comparison reports

promptTemplates.title: Create full-stack agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a full-stack agent with all tools including MCP integrations for complete feature delivery workflows

bestPractices: Start with interview mode to understand agent design principles before using quick mode
Use explicit tool scoping to limit agent capabilities to minimum necessary
Include concrete examples and checklists in agent prompts for consistent behavior
antiPatterns: Avoid creating agents with overly broad tool access
Do not skip defining clear boundaries and DO/DO NOT guidelines
Avoid vague responsibilities - be specific about agent workflow steps

faq.question: What tools does this skill require?
faq.answer: No tools required. This is a documentation-only skill that guides users through creating agent definition files.

faq.question: Can I create agents for any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. Agent prompts are language-agnostic. You define the agent's purpose, tools, and workflow in markdown.

faq.question: How do I integrate created agents with my project?
faq.answer: Place agent definition files in .claude/agents/ directory for project-level sharing or ~/.claude/agents/ for personal use.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads its own documentation files. It does not access project files, credentials, or make network requests.

faq.question: Why is my agent not being recognized?
faq.answer: Check file location (.claude/agents/), file extension (.md), frontmatter name matches filename, and valid YAML syntax.

faq.question: How does this compare to built-in agents?
faq.answer: Built-in agents (general-purpose, explore, plan) are fixed. Custom agents let you define specific purposes, tools, prompts, and behaviors.
